nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

type.description: The type represents the zone of trust or scope for the analyzer.

createdAt.description: A timestamp for the time at which the analyzer was created.

lastResourceAnalyzed.description: The resource that was most recently analyzed by the analyzer.

lastResourceAnalyzedAt.description: The time at which the most recently analyzed resource was analyzed.

tags.description:
An array of key-value pairs applied to the analyzer. The key-value pairs consist of the set of Unicode letters, digits, whitespace, _, ., /, =, +, and -.

The tag key is a value that is 1 to 128 characters in length and cannot be prefixed with aws:.

The tag value is a value that is 0 to 256 characters in length.

status.description: The status of the analyzer. An Active analyzer successfully monitors supported resources and generates new findings. The analyzer is Disabled when a user action, such as removing trusted access for Identity and Access Management Access Analyzer from Organizations, causes the analyzer to stop generating new findings. The status is Creating when the analyzer creation is in progress and Failed when the analyzer creation has failed.

statusReason.description: The statusReason provides more details about the current status of the analyzer. For example, if the creation for the analyzer fails, a Failed status is returned. For an analyzer with organization as the type, this failure can be due to an issue with creating the service-linked roles required in the member accounts of the Amazon Web Services organization.

configuration.description: Specifies if the analyzer is an external access, unused access, or internal access analyzer. The GetAnalyzer action includes this property in its response if a configuration is specified, while the ListAnalyzers action omits it.

policyGenerations.description: A PolicyGeneration object that contains details about the generated policy.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

jobId.description: The JobId that is returned by the StartPolicyGeneration operation. The JobId can be used with GetGeneratedPolicy to retrieve the generated policies or used with CancelPolicyGeneration to cancel the policy generation request.

principalArn.description: The ARN of the IAM entity (user or role) for which you are generating a policy.

status.description: The status of the policy generation request.

startedOn.description: A timestamp of when the policy generation started.

completedOn.description: A timestamp of when the policy generation was completed.

result.description: The result of the check for whether the access is allowed. If the result is PASS, the specified policy doesn't allow any of the specified permissions in the access object. If the result is FAIL, the specified policy might allow some or all of the permissions in the access object.

message.description: The message indicating whether the specified access is allowed.

reasons.description: A description of the reasoning of the result.

description.description: A description of the reasoning of a result of checking for access.

statementIndex.description: The index number of the reason statement.

result.description: The result of the check for new access. If the result is PASS, no new access is allowed by the updated policy. If the result is FAIL, the updated policy might allow new access.

message.description: The message indicating whether the updated policy allows new access.

reasons.description: A description of the reasoning of the result.

description.description: A description of the reasoning of a result of checking for access.

statementIndex.description: The index number of the reason statement.

result.description: The result of the check for public access to the specified resource type. If the result is PASS, the policy doesn't allow public access to the specified resource type. If the result is FAIL, the policy might allow public access to the specified resource type.

message.description: The message indicating whether the specified policy allows public access to resources.

reasons.description: A list of reasons why the specified resource policy grants public access for the resource type.

description.description: A description of the reasoning of a result of checking for access.

statementIndex.description: The index number of the reason statement.

id.description: The unique ID for the access preview.

arn.description: The ARN of the analyzer that was created by the request.

accessPreview.description: An object that contains information about the access preview.

id.description: The unique ID for the access preview.

analyzerArn.description: The ARN of the analyzer used to generate the access preview.

configurations.description: A map of resource ARNs for the proposed resource configuration.

createdAt.description: The time at which the access preview was created.

status.description:
The status of the access preview.

  • Creating - The access preview creation is in progress.

  • Completed - The access preview is complete. You can preview findings for external access to the resource.

  • Failed - The access preview creation has failed.

statusReason.description:
Provides more details about the current status of the access preview.

For example, if the creation of the access preview fails, a Failed status is returned. This failure can be due to an internal issue with the analysis or due to an invalid resource configuration.

resource.description: An AnalyzedResource object that contains information that IAM Access Analyzer found when it analyzed the resource.

resourceArn.description: The ARN of the resource that was analyzed.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource that was analyzed.

createdAt.description: The time at which the finding was created.

analyzedAt.description: The time at which the resource was analyzed.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the finding was updated.

isPublic.description: Indicates whether the policy that generated the finding grants public access to the resource.

actions.description: The actions that an external principal is granted permission to use by the policy that generated the finding.

sharedVia.description: Indicates how the access that generated the finding is granted. This is populated for Amazon S3 bucket findings.

status.description: The current status of the finding generated from the analyzed resource.

resourceOwnerAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the resource.

analyzer.description: An AnalyzerSummary object that contains information about the analyzer.

type.description: The type represents the zone of trust or scope for the analyzer.

createdAt.description: A timestamp for the time at which the analyzer was created.

lastResourceAnalyzed.description: The resource that was most recently analyzed by the analyzer.

lastResourceAnalyzedAt.description: The time at which the most recently analyzed resource was analyzed.

tags.description:
An array of key-value pairs applied to the analyzer. The key-value pairs consist of the set of Unicode letters, digits, whitespace, _, ., /, =, +, and -.

The tag key is a value that is 1 to 128 characters in length and cannot be prefixed with aws:.

The tag value is a value that is 0 to 256 characters in length.

status.description: The status of the analyzer. An Active analyzer successfully monitors supported resources and generates new findings. The analyzer is Disabled when a user action, such as removing trusted access for Identity and Access Management Access Analyzer from Organizations, causes the analyzer to stop generating new findings. The status is Creating when the analyzer creation is in progress and Failed when the analyzer creation has failed.

statusReason.description: The statusReason provides more details about the current status of the analyzer. For example, if the creation for the analyzer fails, a Failed status is returned. For an analyzer with organization as the type, this failure can be due to an issue with creating the service-linked roles required in the member accounts of the Amazon Web Services organization.

configuration.description: Specifies if the analyzer is an external access, unused access, or internal access analyzer. The GetAnalyzer action includes this property in its response if a configuration is specified, while the ListAnalyzers action omits it.

archiveRule.description: Contains information about an archive rule. Archive rules automatically archive new findings that meet the criteria you define when you create the rule.

filter.description: A filter used to define the archive rule.

createdAt.description: The time at which the archive rule was created.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the archive rule was last updated.

principal.description: The external principal that has access to a resource within the zone of trust.

action.description: The action in the analyzed policy statement that an external principal has permission to use.

resource.description: The resource that an external principal has access to.

isPublic.description: Indicates whether the policy that generated the finding allows public access to the resource.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource identified in the finding.

condition.description: The condition in the analyzed policy statement that resulted in a finding.

createdAt.description: The time at which the finding was generated.

analyzedAt.description: The time at which the resource was analyzed.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the finding was updated.

resourceOwnerAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the resource.

sources.description: The sources of the finding. This indicates how the access that generated the finding is granted. It is populated for Amazon S3 bucket findings.

resourceControlPolicyRestriction.description: The type of restriction applied to the finding by the resource owner with an Organizations resource control policy (RCP).

startedAt.description: The time at which the retrieval of the finding recommendation was started.

completedAt.description: The time at which the retrieval of the finding recommendation was completed.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

error.description: Detailed information about the reason that the retrieval of a recommendation for the finding failed.

resourceArn.description: The ARN of the resource of the finding.

recommendedSteps.description: A group of recommended steps for the finding.

recommendationType.description: The type of recommendation for the finding.

status.description: The status of the retrieval of the finding recommendation.

unusedPermissionsRecommendedStep.description: A recommended step for an unused permissions finding.

analyzedAt.description: The time at which the resource-based policy or IAM entity that generated the finding was analyzed.

createdAt.description: The time at which the finding was created.

id.description: The ID of the finding to retrieve.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource identified in the finding.

resourceOwnerAccount.description: Tye Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the resource.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the finding was updated.

findingDetails.description: A localized message that explains the finding and provides guidance on how to address it.

findingType.description: The type of the finding. For external access analyzers, the type is ExternalAccess. For unused access analyzers, the type can be UnusedIAMRole, UnusedIAMUserAccessKey, UnusedIAMUserPassword, or UnusedPermission. For internal access analyzers, the type is InternalAccess.

internalAccessDetails.description: The details for an internal access analyzer finding. This contains information about access patterns identified within your Amazon Web Services organization or account.

externalAccessDetails.description: The details for an external access analyzer finding.

unusedPermissionDetails.description: The details for an unused access analyzer finding with an unused permission finding type.

unusedIamUserAccessKeyDetails.description: The details for an unused access analyzer finding with an unused IAM user access key finding type.

unusedIamRoleDetails.description: The details for an unused access analyzer finding with an unused IAM role finding type.

unusedIamUserPasswordDetails.description: The details for an unused access analyzer finding with an unused IAM user password finding type.

findingsStatistics.description: A group of external access or unused access findings statistics.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The time at which the retrieval of the findings statistics was last updated. If the findings statistics have not been previously retrieved for the specified analyzer, this field will not be populated.

externalAccessFindingsStatistics.description: The aggregate statistics for an external access analyzer.

internalAccessFindingsStatistics.description: The aggregate statistics for an internal access analyzer. This includes information about active, archived, and resolved findings related to internal access within your Amazon Web Services organization or account.

unusedAccessFindingsStatistics.description: The aggregate statistics for an unused access analyzer.

jobDetails.description: A GeneratedPolicyDetails object that contains details about the generated policy.

generatedPolicyResult.description: A GeneratedPolicyResult object that contains the generated policies and associated details.

jobId.description: The JobId that is returned by the StartPolicyGeneration operation. The JobId can be used with GetGeneratedPolicy to retrieve the generated policies or used with CancelPolicyGeneration to cancel the policy generation request.

startedOn.description: A timestamp of when the job was started.

completedOn.description: A timestamp of when the job was completed.

jobError.description: The job error for the policy generation request.

findings.description: A list of access preview findings that match the specified filter criteria.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

id.description: The ID of the access preview finding. This ID uniquely identifies the element in the list of access preview findings and is not related to the finding ID in Access Analyzer.

existingFindingId.description: The existing ID of the finding in IAM Access Analyzer, provided only for existing findings.

existingFindingStatus.description: The existing status of the finding, provided only for existing findings.

principal.description: The external principal that has access to a resource within the zone of trust.

action.description: The action in the analyzed policy statement that an external principal has permission to perform.

condition.description: The condition in the analyzed policy statement that resulted in a finding.

resource.description: The resource that an external principal has access to. This is the resource associated with the access preview.

isPublic.description: Indicates whether the policy that generated the finding allows public access to the resource.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource that can be accessed in the finding.

createdAt.description: The time at which the access preview finding was created.

changeType.description:
Provides context on how the access preview finding compares to existing access identified in IAM Access Analyzer.

  • New - The finding is for newly-introduced access.

  • Unchanged - The preview finding is an existing finding that would remain unchanged.

  • Changed - The preview finding is an existing finding with a change in status.

For example, a Changed finding with preview status Resolved and existing status Active indicates the existing Active finding would become Resolved as a result of the proposed permissions change.

status.description: The preview status of the finding. This is what the status of the finding would be after permissions deployment. For example, a Changed finding with preview status Resolved and existing status Active indicates the existing Active finding would become Resolved as a result of the proposed permissions change.

resourceOwnerAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the resource. For most Amazon Web Services resources, the owning account is the account in which the resource was created.

sources.description: The sources of the finding. This indicates how the access that generated the finding is granted. It is populated for Amazon S3 bucket findings.

resourceControlPolicyRestriction.description: The type of restriction applied to the finding by the resource owner with an Organizations resource control policy (RCP).

accessPreviews.description: A list of access previews retrieved for the analyzer.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

id.description: The unique ID for the access preview.

analyzerArn.description: The ARN of the analyzer used to generate the access preview.

createdAt.description: The time at which the access preview was created.

status.description:
The status of the access preview.

  • Creating - The access preview creation is in progress.

  • Completed - The access preview is complete and previews the findings for external access to the resource.

  • Failed - The access preview creation has failed.

statusReason.description: Provides more details about the current status of the access preview. For example, if the creation of the access preview fails, a Failed status is returned. This failure can be due to an internal issue with the analysis or due to an invalid proposed resource configuration.

analyzedResources.description: A list of resources that were analyzed.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

resourceOwnerAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the resource.

resourceType.description: The type of resource that was analyzed.

archiveRules.description: A list of archive rules created for the specified analyzer.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

filter.description: A filter used to define the archive rule.

createdAt.description: The time at which the archive rule was created.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the archive rule was last updated.

findings.description: A list of findings retrieved from the analyzer that match the filter criteria specified, if any.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

principal.description: The external principal that has access to a resource within the zone of trust.

action.description: The action in the analyzed policy statement that an external principal has permission to use.

resource.description: The resource that the external principal has access to.

isPublic.description: Indicates whether the finding reports a resource that has a policy that allows public access.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource that the external principal has access to.

condition.description: The condition in the analyzed policy statement that resulted in a finding.

createdAt.description: The time at which the finding was created.

analyzedAt.description: The time at which the resource-based policy that generated the finding was analyzed.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the finding was most recently updated.

resourceOwnerAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the resource.

error.description: The error that resulted in an Error finding.

sources.description: The sources of the finding. This indicates how the access that generated the finding is granted. It is populated for Amazon S3 bucket findings.

resourceControlPolicyRestriction.description: The type of restriction applied to the finding by the resource owner with an Organizations resource control policy (RCP).

findings.description: A list of findings retrieved from the analyzer that match the filter criteria specified, if any.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

analyzedAt.description: The time at which the resource-based policy or IAM entity that generated the finding was analyzed.

createdAt.description: The time at which the finding was created.

error.description: The error that resulted in an Error finding.

resource.description: The resource that the external principal has access to.

resourceType.description: The type of the resource that the external principal has access to.

resourceOwnerAccount.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the resource.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the finding was most recently updated.

findingType.description: The type of the access finding. For external access analyzers, the type is ExternalAccess. For unused access analyzers, the type can be UnusedIAMRole, UnusedIAMUserAccessKey, UnusedIAMUserPassword, or UnusedPermission. For internal access analyzers, the type is InternalAccess.

tags.description: The tags that are applied to the specified resource.

jobId.description: The JobId that is returned by the StartPolicyGeneration operation. The JobId can be used with GetGeneratedPolicy to retrieve the generated policies or used with CancelPolicyGeneration to cancel the policy generation request.

configuration.description: Contains information about the configuration of an analyzer for an Amazon Web Services organization or account.

unusedAccess.description: Specifies the configuration of an unused access analyzer for an Amazon Web Services organization or account.

internalAccess.description: Specifies the configuration of an internal access analyzer for an Amazon Web Services organization or account. This configuration determines how the analyzer evaluates access within your Amazon Web Services environment.

findings.description: The list of findings in a policy returned by IAM Access Analyzer based on its suite of policy checks.

nextToken.description: A token used for pagination of results returned.

findingDetails.description: A localized message that explains the finding and provides guidance on how to address it.

findingType.description:
The impact of the finding.

Security warnings report when the policy allows access that we consider overly permissive.

Errors report when a part of the policy is not functional.

Warnings report non-security issues when a policy does not conform to policy writing best practices.

Suggestions recommend stylistic improvements in the policy that do not impact access.

issueCode.description: The issue code provides an identifier of the issue associated with this finding.

learnMoreLink.description: A link to additional documentation about the type of finding.

locations.description: The list of locations in the policy document that are related to the finding. The issue code provides a summary of an issue identified by the finding.
